summary:
The activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT) directly measures deficiencies in intrinsically active factors such as XIX, XI, IX, and VIII within the coagulation cascade. It uniquely detects abnormalities affecting both the intrinsic and common pathways due to its specific design targeting those components.
